title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the initial speed, we need to consider the projectile motion of the golf ball. The ball is launched at an angle of $$40$$ degrees with respect to the horizontal, and we need to find the speed that allows it to travel the given horizontal and vertical distances.

title: Breaking Down the Motion
text: The motion can be broken down into horizontal and vertical components. Use trigonometry to find these components based on the launch angle and initial speed.

title: Horizontal Component of Velocity
text: The horizontal component of the velocity can be found using the formula: $$v_x=v cos\left(\theta\right)$$. What is $$v_x$$ in terms of v?

title: Vertical Component of Velocity
text: The vertical component of the velocity can be found using the formula: $$v_y=v sin\left(\theta\right)$$. What is $$v_y$$ in terms of v?

title: Using Kinematic Equations
text: Use the kinematic equations to relate the vertical motion to the initial vertical velocity, the height, and the acceleration due to gravity.

title: Vertical Motion Equation
text: The vertical motion can be described by the equation: $$y=v_y t-0.5g t^2$$. Use this to find the time of flight in terms of v.

title: Relating Horizontal Motion
text: The horizontal distance traveled is related to the horizontal velocity and the time of flight. Use this to find the initial speed.

title: Horizontal Motion Equation
text: The horizontal motion can be described by the equation: $$x=v_x t$$. Use this to solve for v.

title: Solving for Initial Speed
text: Substitute the known values and solve the equation to find the initial speed.

title: Final Calculation
text: Calculate the initial speed using the equation derived. Remember to use $$g=\frac{9.8m}{s^2}$$.
